title: Conditions of Contract for Construction (Red Book), 2nd Edition
scope: Employer-designed Building & Engineering Works under Engineer administration. Most-cited Sub-Clauses: 4.20 (Progress Reports), 4.21 (program/records under earlier numbering — see Notes), 8.4 (Advance Warning), 8.5 (Extension of Time for Completion, incl. concurrent delay), 8.7 (Delay Damages), 8.8 (Suspension), 13 (Variations), 17 (Care of the Works/Indemnities), 20.1 (Notice of Claim — 28-day condition precedent) and 20.2 (Claims procedure incl. 84-day fully-detailed claim).

title: Conditions of Contract for Construction (Red Book), 1st Edition
scope: Predecessor still widely incorporated in legacy and MDB-financed contracts. Most-cited Sub-Clauses: 4.21 (Progress Reports), 8.3 (Programme), 8.4 (Extension of Time for Completion), 8.7 (Delay Damages), 13.1-13.3 (Variations & Value Engineering), 20.1 (Contractor's Claims — 28-day notice condition precedent).

title: Conditions of Contract for Plant and Design-Build (Yellow Book), 2nd Edition
scope: Contractor-designed Plant and Design-Build (E&M plus building/engineering). Most-cited Sub-Clauses: 4.20 (Progress Reports), 5 (Design), 8.4 (Advance Warning), 8.5 (Extension of Time for Completion), 8.7 (Delay Damages), 9 (Tests on Completion), 11 (Defects), 12 (Tests after Completion), 13 (Variations), 20.1/20.2 (Claims — 28-day notice, 84-day fully-detailed claim).

title: Conditions of Contract for Plant and Design-Build (Yellow Book), 1st Edition
scope: Legacy Design-Build form still used in many MENA and MDB contracts. Most-cited Sub-Clauses: 4.21, 5.1-5.2 (Contractor's design obligations), 8.3, 8.4 (EOT), 13 (Variations), 20.1 (Claims notice).

title: Conditions of Contract for EPC/Turnkey Projects (Silver Book), 2nd Edition
scope: Turnkey EPC with single-point Contractor risk allocation, no Engineer (Employer's Representative). Most-cited Sub-Clauses: 4.12 (Unforeseeable Difficulties — narrower risk transfer than Red/Yellow), 5 (Design), 8.4 (Advance Warning), 8.5 (EOT), 8.8 (Delay Damages), 13 (Variations), 17 (Care/Indemnities), 20.1/20.2 (Claims).

title: Short Form of Contract (Green Book), 2nd Edition
scope: Lower-value or short-duration works (now significantly expanded vs the 1999 first edition — closer in structure to Red/Yellow 1999). Cited for: simplified claims & dispute procedure, design responsibility flexibility, programme and progress reporting requirements.

title: Conditions of Contract for Design, Build and Operate Projects (Gold Book), 1st Edition
scope: Single-contract green-field DBO with 20-year operation service period. Pioneered the NEC-style 'advance warning' obligation later adopted into the 2017 Red/Yellow/Silver Sub-Clause 8.4. Cited for: integration of design-build with long-term O&M, dispute avoidance, asset-replacement schedule.

title: A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK Guide) — Seventh Edition AND The Standard for Project Management
publisher: Project Management Institute (PMI), Newtown Square, PA
scope: Principles-based PM standard structured around 12 Project Management Principles and 8 Project Performance Domains: Stakeholders, Team, Development Approach & Life Cycle, Planning, Project Work, Delivery, Measurement, Uncertainty. Includes Tailoring and Models/Methods/Artifacts sections.

title: A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK Guide) — Sixth Edition
publisher: Project Management Institute (PMI), Newtown Square, PA
scope: Process-based PM standard, still widely referenced. 5 Process Groups (Initiating, Planning, Executing, Monitoring & Controlling, Closing) x 10 Knowledge Areas (Integration, Scope, Schedule, Cost, Quality, Resource, Communications, Risk, Procurement, Stakeholder) = 49 processes with ITTOs. Includes the Agile Practice Guide as companion.

title: ISO 21502:2020 — Project, programme and portfolio management — Guidance on project management
scope: Replaced ISO 21500:2012. Generic, organisation-independent guidance on project management practices (integrated, sustainable, controlled, life-cycle-based). Used as the international neutral counterpart/benchmark to PMBOK.

title: ISO 9001:2015 — Quality management systems — Requirements
latestEdition: 5th Edition 2015 (revision under DIS — publication expected September 2026)
scope: Requirements for a QMS — leadership, planning, support, operation, performance evaluation, improvement. High-Level Structure (Annex SL) used as the basis for project quality plans, ITPs, NCR/CAR systems on Sigma PMO engagements.

title: ISO 31000:2018 — Risk management — Guidelines
scope: Principles, framework and process for risk management. Underpins the Sigma PMO risk register methodology (risk identification, analysis, evaluation, treatment, monitoring, communication). Companion: ISO/IEC 31010 (risk assessment techniques).

title: ISO 19650-1:2018 — BIM Information management — Part 1: Concepts and principles
scope: Concepts and principles for managing information across the asset life-cycle using BIM: information requirements (OIR, AIR, PIR, EIR), CDE, ISO 19650 information container model.

title: ISO 19650-2:2018 — BIM Information management — Part 2: Delivery phase of the assets
scope: Requirements for the information management process during the delivery phase: appointment workflow, BEP, MIDP/TIDP, information delivery milestones. Basis of UK BIM Framework guidance and most GCC BIM mandates (UAE, KSA).

title: ISO 19650-3:2020 — BIM Information management — Part 3: Operational phase of the assets
scope: Information management during asset operation/maintenance. Replaces PAS 1192-3. Used for AIM (Asset Information Model) handover and FM integration.

title: ISO 19650-5:2020 — Security-minded approach to information management
latestEdition: 1st Edition 2020 (some sources cite 2021 publication — verify against ISO catalogue for procurement)
scope: Security-minded approach to information management for sensitive assets. Replaces PAS 1192-5.

title: ISO 16739-1:2024 — Industry Foundation Classes (IFC 4.3) for data sharing in the construction and facility management industries
latestEdition: ISO 16739-1:2024 (IFC 4.3.2.0); buildingSMART announced ISO ratification January 2024
publisher: ISO, Geneva — schema authored by buildingSMART International
scope: Open, vendor-neutral data schema for buildings AND now infrastructure (rail, road, bridge, ports, tunnels). >1300 entities, ~2500 properties in >750 sets. Mandatory exchange format in most GCC government BIM mandates.

title: BIMForum Level of Development (LOD) Specification — 2023 Edition
publisher: BIMForum (a forum of AGC of America and AIA TAP)
scope: Definitions and illustrations for LOD 100/200/300/350/400/500 across building systems. Aligned with AIA E201-2022 BIM Exhibit. Most-cited US/global LOD reference.

title: AACE International Total Cost Management Framework — 2nd Edition
latestEdition: 2nd Edition 2015; ISBN 9781508804765; ed. H. Lance Stephenson, CCP FAACE
scope: Umbrella framework integrating cost engineering across portfolio/program/project life-cycle using a PDCA process map. Parent document referenced by every numbered AACE Recommended Practice.

title: AACE RP 17R-97 — Cost Estimate Classification System
scope: Generic 5-class estimate classification matrix (Class 5 concept screening through Class 1 definitive). Parent RP for the 'as applied in' series (18R-97 process industries, 47R-11 mining, 56R-08 building/general construction, 96R-18 hydropower, etc.).

title: AACE RP 14R-90 — Required Skills and Knowledge of Project Cost Estimating (also covers Planning & Scheduling Professional skills set; verify which RP you intend)
scope: Defines core skill sets that underpin AACE certifications (CCP, PSP). Note: there is some inconsistency in user-facing references — confirm against the AACE recommended-practice index before citing the title verbatim.

title: AACE RP 29R-03 — Forensic Schedule Analysis
scope: Nine CPM forensic methodologies (MIP 3.1–3.9: As-Planned vs As-Built, Impacted As-Planned, Collapsed As-Built, Time Impact Analysis, Windows, etc.) plus 11 selection factors. The de-facto global reference for delay claim analysis alongside the SCL Delay & Disruption Protocol.

title: AACE RP 32R-04 — Determining Activity Durations
scope: Three-phase guideline for setting activity durations: unconstrained, constraint-adjusted, and revised for project requirements. Maps to TCM 7.2 (Planning).

title: AACE RP 49R-06 — Identifying the Critical Path
scope: Guideline for correctly identifying and validating the critical path within a CPM schedule — total float, longest path, multi-calendar effects, near-critical activities.

title: AACE RP 38R-06 — Documenting the Schedule Basis
scope: Standard outline for a Schedule Basis Document (assumptions, exclusions, calendars, constraints, resource loading rules, risks) — the backbone of defensible baselines and any later forensic claim.

title: Oracle Primavera P6 EPPM User Guide (Version 24)
latestEdition: Version 24 (current); Version 22 / Version 20 still in use at clients
scope: Authoritative reference for P6 EPPM features: EPS/OBS/WBS structures, activity types, relationships and lags, calendars, resource/role assignment, leveling, baselines, claim digger, Global Change, reflection projects, XER/XML import-export, P6 Visualizer.

title: Oracle Primavera P6 Professional User Guide
scope: Standalone P6 Professional client guide — the offline desktop tool most contractors use. Cite by document title, not by paywalled URL (Oracle support requires login for some artifacts).
